summary.task: MInDS-14 en-US tool/intent identification via FROZEN bi-encoder verifiable-reward selection
summary.mechanism: SentenceTransformer(omni-embed-nemotron-3b) cosine Acc@1 selection over static text candidate-cards; NO weight updates, NO generation/decoding, NO remote API. Training-free verifiable-reward SELECTION (Operator-A-class embedding reranking), not generative best-of-N.
summary.verdict: REAL, LOCAL, reproducible content/intent gain from a single frozen model under a verifiable reward: the candidate-card policy arm reaches Acc@1=0.984, +0.126 [0.077,0.181] over the raw-schema baseline (paired bootstrap, CI excludes 0). This corroborates the paper's 0.852->0.984 (~+0.13) MInDS number with a committed artifact and a paired CI. It is a FROZEN BI-ENCODER SELECTION result, NOT a generative Operator-B best-of-N result.
summary.provenance_note: Regenerated in the POMDP restructure (Step 1 provenance pilot) to (a) commit a reproducible artifact where none existed and (b) verify the flagged MInDS number. The paper must re-attribute the mechanism (bi-encoder verifiable selection, not generative best-of-N) and label the baseline as the raw-schema arm (0.857), not 'bare intent-name'.
